verdict.comprehension: Every load-bearing argument survives intact - the Jagged Frontier, the four principles, the Just-Me/Delegated/Automated + Centaur/Cyborg taxonomy, how LLMs predict tokens and are pretrained+RLHF'd, the marquee studies (BCG, Wharton, Noy-Zhang, Haase-Hanel, Bloom's 2-sigma), and the four futures, all anchored verbatim with bridges connecting them, so Ram can reconstruct Mollick's whole case from this alone.
verdict.visual_pleasure: Serif body at a 66ch measure with generous leading, drop caps, accent pull-quotes, dark-mode support and a reading-time-per-chapter chrome make it a genuinely pleasant read; the only friction is the long run of stacked full verbatim blockquotes in the heavier chapters (4, 5, 6), where several 200+ word anchors sit back-to-back with thin bridge connective tissue and can feel dense.
verdict.navigation: Sticky chapter TOC + a 'By theme' index + scroll-spy current-section highlighting + a live progress bar with '% and min left' all work and let Ram orient, jump by topic, and resume; minor gaps are no in-page 'back to top' affordance and sidenotes/xref previews that are hover-only (mobile gets a long-press fallback, which is good).
verdict.illustration: All 13 Channel-A graphics earn their place - the LLM-build flowchart, diffusion pipeline, the SVG Jagged Frontier wall with two same-distance tasks on opposite sides, the 2x2 task-taxonomy quadrant, the imitation-machines and four-futures timelines, and the comparison tables (GPT-3.5 vs 4 bar exam, citation hallucination 98%->20%, AI-vs-human creativity, leveling effect) each externalize a relationship Mollick actually argues, every figure cites its source spans, and nothing is decorative.
verdict.books_finished_rate: At ~57 min and 14.2k words this is finishable in a single sitting versus a 250-page book Ram would likely abandon; the diagrams plus verbatim anchors give both a fast skim path and real retention hooks, and the per-chapter time estimates remove the 'how much is left' dread - this would measurably raise both his finish rate AND his recall of Co-Intelligence.

comprehension_test.question: What is the Jagged Frontier of AI, and why does it mean a sonnet and an exactly-fifty-word poem fall on different sides of it?
Name Mollick's four rules (principles) for working with co-intelligence.
Define Just Me, Delegated, and Automated tasks, and contrast a Centaur with a Cyborg way of working with AI.
In the BCG consulting study, what happened to the gap between top and bottom performers once consultants used GPT-4, and what happened on the deliberately off-frontier task?
Technically, what is an LLM doing when it answers, and what are pretraining, weights, and RLHF?
What are the four future scenarios Mollick lays out, ordered by speed of AI capability growth?

improvement_note: In the dense concept chapters (4, 5, 6) break the longest stacked verbatim blockquotes with one extra bridge sentence or a subhead so the eye gets relief between back-to-back 200-word anchors.
